To fix the Command Injection vulnerability (CWE-78) in `@coding-solo/godot-mcp`, we need to avoid passing user-controlled input to a shell. The `child_process.exec()` function spawns a shell by default, which evaluates shell metacharacters (like `$()`, `&`, `|`, `;`) and leads to command injection.

The safest and most standard way to fix this in Node.js is to replace `exec()` with `execFile()`.
